# content.md — Final Website Copy
**Site:** Single-page portfolio — Teevin "Atom" Hiranlertprasert, UX/UI & Visual Designer, Bangkok
**Author:** Content Writer · **For:** Frontend developer — drop this copy into the structure defined in design-spec.md §3–4.
**Rule for the developer:** use this file for ALL copy. Facts here match the client-approved case study (`smart-utilities.html`). Do not edit numbers or names.

---

## 1. Meta (index.html `<head>`)

- **`<title>`:** `Atom — UX/UI & Visual Designer in Bangkok`
- **Meta description:** `Portfolio of Teevin "Atom" Hiranlertprasert, a Bangkok-based UX/UI and visual designer with 10+ years across product design, branding and campaigns.`

---

## 2. Nav

- **Wordmark:** `ATOM®` (use the same wordmark on `smart-utilities.html`)

## 3. Hero

- **Eyebrow:** `UX/UI & VISUAL DESIGNER — BANGKOK`
- **Display headline (client-specified, 2026-07-03):** `Where insights meet empathy, ensuring every data point drives business success and user happiness.`

## 4. About (`#about`)

- **Eyebrow:** `ABOUT`
- **Section heading (H1):** `Design that ships.`

**Paragraph 1:**
`I'm Atom — Teevin Hiranlertprasert — a designer based in Bangkok. I work across the full spectrum: product UX, interface systems, brand identities and campaign visuals. My projects range from government platforms and telecom tools to NFT games and coffee brands.`

**Paragraph 2:**
`I care about the unglamorous parts: information architecture, Thai and English typography, edge cases and handoff. Good design is not the prettiest version. It is the version that actually ships.`

**Stat row (number + label):**

| Number | Label |
|---|---|
| `10+` | `Years experience` |
| `18` | `Selected projects` |
| `4` | `Disciplines` |
| `∞` | `Coffee` |

## 5. Services (`#services`)

- **Eyebrow:** `SERVICES`
- **Section heading (H1):** `What I do`

**Card 1 — UX/UI Design**
- Description: `Research, user flows, wireframes and polished interfaces for web and mobile. From civic platforms to point-of-sale tools.`
- Tags: `Dashboards` · `Mobile apps` · `Design systems`

**Card 2 — Branding**
- Description: `Identity systems from logo to packaging. Built to survive the real world, not just the pitch deck.`
- Tags: `Logo` · `Identity` · `Packaging`

**Card 3 — Graphic Design**
- Description: `Editorial, print and exhibition graphics with typographic care. Bilingual layouts that hold up in Thai and English.`
- Tags: `Editorial` · `Print` · `KV`

**Card 4 — Marketing & Social**
- Description: `Campaign key visuals and social content for national brands. Made to perform, on schedule, at volume.`
- Tags: `Campaigns` · `Social` · `Content`

## 6. Work (`#work`) — all 18 projects

- **Eyebrow:** `SELECTED WORK`
- **Section heading (H1):** `Featured projects`
- **Filter labels (exact):** `All` · `UX/UI Design` · `Branding` · `Graphic Design` · `Marketing & Social`

Format per project: **card tagline** (one line, ≤ 110 chars, shown under the title) + **modal description** (2–3 sentences, shown in the lightbox).

### 1. Baania — UX/UI Design
- **Tagline:** `Full UX/UI for Thailand's real-estate marketplace, from search to price estimates.`
- **Modal description:** `Baania helps Thai home-seekers search, compare and value property. I designed the full experience: search and listings, price-estimate tools, chat, and analytics dashboards. The system covers desktop and mobile with one consistent interface language.`

### 2. Smart Utilities — UX/UI Design
> **Developer note:** this card does NOT open the modal. It links to the case study page `smart-utilities.html` and carries a `Case study ↗` badge.
- **Tagline:** `Municipal utility platform that grew one district's revenue from ฿0.9M to ฿8M a month.`
- **Card/link description (if shown anywhere):** `As Lead UX/UI Designer (2020–2022), I designed three connected products: an operations dashboard, a citizen payment portal and an OCR meter-reading handheld app. In Koh Samui, monthly revenue grew 8.9× — from ฿900,000 to ฿8 million — with no added staff. Read the full case study.`

### 3. TILOG Virtual Expo — UX/UI Design
- **Tagline:** `Online business-matching platform for the TILOG-LOGISTIX trade event.`
- **Modal description:** `When trade shows moved online, TILOG-LOGISTIX needed a way for buyers and exhibitors to still meet. I designed the business-matching platform: company discovery, meeting scheduling and built-in video conferencing. The goal was simple — make a virtual expo feel organised, not chaotic.`

### 4. True iService — UX/UI Design
- **Tagline:** `Tablet POS app that guides telecom staff through a complete SIM sale in one flow.`
- **Modal description:** `A tablet point-of-sale app for telecom retail staff. It guides a full SIM sale order — package selection, catalog browsing and identity verification — in one continuous flow. Fewer steps for staff means shorter queues for customers.`

### 5. M-Culture Big Data — UX/UI Design
- **Tagline:** `Big-data platform UI for the Ministry of Culture, where Thai motifs meet modern layout.`
- **Modal description:** `Landing page and service UI for the Ministry of Culture's big-data platform. The design blends traditional Thai pattern motifs with a clean, modern layout. It shows government data can look both official and inviting.`

### 6. K-FLO — UX/UI Design
- **Tagline:** `Bilingual corporate website for an industrial filtration brand, responsive on every screen.`
- **Modal description:** `A corporate website for K-FLO, an industrial filtration brand. The site works in two languages and adapts across desktop, tablet and mobile. Product categories and selection flows stay clear even with a technical catalog.`

### 7. Crypto Wushu Game — UX/UI Design
- **Tagline:** `Landing page, character cards and marketplace UI for an NFT martial-arts game.`
- **Modal description:** `Website and game-asset design for an NFT martial-arts game. The work covers the landing page, a five-card character series with ability layouts, and the marketplace UI. Each card had to read as collectible art and as a functional game item.`

### 8. Neue Space Cafe — Branding
- **Tagline:** `A kraft-and-white pattern system carried across logo, packaging and bags.`
- **Modal description:** `A cafe identity built on a kraft-and-white graphic pattern system. The pattern flows from the logo across packaging, cups and bags, so every touchpoint feels like one brand. Simple materials, one consistent visual voice.`

### 9. Korner St. — Branding
- **Tagline:** `Logo and design elements for a neighborhood street-corner brand.`
- **Modal description:** `A compact identity for Korner St., a neighborhood street-corner brand. The logo and its supporting design elements were built to work small, fast and everywhere — from signage to stickers.`

### 10. Ordinary Bangkok — Branding
- **Tagline:** `Minimalist kraft-and-white identity for a Thai homeware label — creatively ordinary.`
- **Modal description:** `Identity and packaging for Ordinary Bangkok, a Thai homeware label. The brand idea is "creatively ordinary": quiet kraft-and-white design that lets the products speak. Restraint here was the whole point.`

### 11. New Economy Academy — Branding
- **Tagline:** `Full identity for a government learning academy — logo, color, graphic style and print.`
- **Modal description:** `A complete identity for New Economy Academy, a government learning academy. The system includes the logo, color palette, graphic style and print collateral such as leaflets. It gives a public institution a fresh, credible face.`

### 12. Craftman Roastery — Branding
- **Tagline:** `Brand and retail graphics for a specialty coffee roaster, from window to exhibition booth.`
- **Modal description:** `Brand and retail graphics for Craftman Roastery, a specialty coffee roaster. The work spans cafe design, a window display and an exhibition booth. One graphic language ties the shop floor to the trade-show stand.`

### 13. Bangkok University Bulletin — Graphic Design
- **Tagline:** `Editorial design for BU International's bulletin, wrapped in a low-poly world map.`
- **Modal description:** `Editorial design for Bangkok University International's bulletin. A low-poly world-map illustration wraps the cover and sets the tone inside. The layout keeps dense student content readable and calm.`

### 14. DCA Book — Graphic Design
- **Tagline:** `Book and poster design for a DCA investment publication, plus launch rewards.`
- **Modal description:** `Book and poster design for a publication on DCA investing. The set includes launch reward materials that extend the cover's visual system. Financial content, presented without the usual clutter.`

### 15. TCEB × Thai Airways — Graphic Design
- **Tagline:** `Leaflet and banner set for the TCEB & TG "Optimice Pass" MICE-travel campaign.`
- **Modal description:** `A leaflet and banner set for the "Optimice Pass" campaign by TCEB and Thai Airways. The materials promote MICE travel with a clear offer and clean layout. Print and banner share one system, so the campaign reads instantly at any size.`

### 16. NT Life Smart Solutions — Graphic Design
- **Tagline:** `Six "Smart" key visuals, brochures and roll-up banners for NT Digital Solutions.`
- **Modal description:** `Campaign collateral for NT Digital Solutions. I designed six "Smart" key visuals — business, education, environment, healthcare, industrial and tracking — plus brochures, leaflets and roll-up banners. One template family keeps six sub-campaigns unmistakably related.`

### 17. CAT Market 4.0 — Marketing & Social
- **Tagline:** `Event campaign for CAT e-business — key visual, roadmap, lucky draw and social series.`
- **Modal description:** `A full event campaign for CAT's e-business market. The set includes the key visual, an activity roadmap, lucky-draw materials and a social post series. Every piece follows the campaign look, from stage backdrop to Facebook feed.`

### 18. Social Media Content — Marketing & Social
- **Tagline:** `Ongoing social content for 7-Eleven, SCG, Arabus, Weber and Thailand Trust Mark.`
- **Modal description:** `Ongoing social content for major brands including 7-Eleven, SCG, Arabus, Weber and Thailand Trust Mark. Each brand keeps its own voice while every post stays feed-ready and on schedule. Consistency at volume is the craft here.`

---

## 7. Process (`#process`)

- **Eyebrow:** `PROCESS`
- **Section heading (H1):** `How I work`

| Step | Title | One sentence |
|---|---|---|
| 01 | `Discover` | `Stakeholder interviews, audits and research into what the business actually needs.` |
| 02 | `Define` | `Information architecture, user flows and the one sentence the design must say.` |
| 03 | `Design` | `From wireframe to pixel-perfect UI or brand system, iterating with the team.` |
| 04 | `Deliver` | `Specs, assets and support through the build — design isn't done until it ships.` |
